ety: S. चखण्ड, intens. fr. rt. खण्ड्+चूर्णं
def: adj. & s.m. Broken to pieces, dashed to pieces, shattered, shivered; racked with pain (the body); small pieces, scraps, atoms, filings
more: adj. 1. Dashed to pieces; bruised; mutilated; broken in may pieces. 1. Chaknā-chūr bhaī, chaknā-chūr, Bahū nauraṅgī, khasam majūr . Boys 'rhyme. Ground to dust, friend, ground to dust! The wife in colours, slave he must. 2. Haḍḍī chaknā-chūr ho gaī . 2. Racked with pain; having pains in every joint. Chalat chalat merī pasrī pirānī, Deh hoë gaī chaknā-chūr, ham pe chalo nāhīṅ jāë. Naiharbā kitnī dūr? ham pe chalo nāhīṅ jāë . E. Song. Ṭhumrī. chaknā-chūr k. , v. a. To shatter; shiver; break to pieces. chaknā-chūr h. , v. n. To be shattered, etc. See chaknā-chūr . Khudā jāne ki hāthā-pāī-kar kis se laṛī, kokā, Ki us ne chūṛīyān kīṅ apnī chaknā chūr mele meṅ . Wom. Raṅgīṅ. God knows with what gallant you 've closed and scuffled, how Else, foster-sister, broken to bits your bangles so?
s. m. Scraps, small pieces, atoms, filings. چکنا چور کرنا chaknā-chūr karnā, To shatter, to shiver, to break to pieces. چکنا چورهونا chaknā-chūr honā, To be dashed to pieces, to shatter.
subs: v.t. To break or dash to pieces, to shatter, shiver

def: s.m. (dialec.) = ćakr, q.v.
s.m. The Bartavelle or Greek partridge, Perdix rufa, or Tetrao rufus (fabled to subsist upon moon-beams, and to eat fire at the full moon).
more: n. m. S. चकोर The Indian red-legged partridge; caccabis chukor . ☞ It is said to be enamoured of the moon and to eat fire at the full moon. It is found all over the Himalayas from Kashmir to Nepal, not extending to Sikkhim, and prefers rocky hill with scrub jungle. The hen lays from 10 to 15 eggs.
s. m. The Bartavelle or Greek partridge (Perdix chukar: Gould. Perdix rufa: Lath. Pers. کبک) said to be enamoured of the moon and to eat fire at the full moon.

def: s.m. Wheel (of a cart, &c.), a potter's wheel; a catharine-wheel; a discus or sharp circular missile weapon; a quoit; an oil-mill; a circle, a ring; circumference; a circular road or course; a circular position; a form of military array (in a circle = ćakābū); circular flight (of a bird, &c.); revolving in a circle, revolution, whirl; round, circuit; circumambulation; a whirlwind; a whirlpool, an eddy, anything revolving in a circle; a whirligig; the lounge (in a manege); giddiness, vertigo; — an army, a multitude; a side, a quarter (e.g. ćahoṅ cakkar-meṅ, 'On all four sides'); (in Gram.) a table or paradigm; (in Astron.) the zodiac with its twelve signs; (fig.) maze, perplexity; misfortune, scrape
more: n. m. S. चक्र a circle. 1. A wheel; disk; discus; quoit. 2. A circle (A. dāërā ); circumference (A. muhīt ); a circular road. 3. An eddy; a whirlwind; whirlpool. 4. Catharine wheels. 5. A round; circuit; circumbendibus; sweep. Tīn kos kā chakkar khāyā . 6. A whirl; rapid rotation or circumvolution; gyration. 7. Circumambulation; roving; vagrancy. Is ke pāṅo meṅ chakkar hai . He is always roving. 8. The course of time. 9. Giddiness; vertigo; swoon. 10. Perplexity; difficulty; maze; scrape; fix. chakkar bāṅdhnā , v. a. To whirl rapidly round. chakr-chāl , n. f. S. चक्र गत्ति ( Mech .) Rotation; rotatory motion. chakr-chhūtī lakīr ; A. mumas-i-dāëra , n. m. ( Math .) A tangent. chakkar-adh-kaṭ ; A. qutr-i-dāëra , n. m. S. केन्द्र ( Math .) Diameter. chakkar-bīch ; A markaz-i-dāëra , n. m. ( Math .) The centre of a circle. chakkar-ṭukṛā ; A. qaus-i-dāërā , n. m. S. वृत्त खंड ( Math .) A segm…
n. m. A mug; a small earthen vessel.

s. m. 1. A whirlwind or whirlpool. 2. A discus or sharp circular missile weapon. (It was with this instrument, called सुदर्शनचक्र that Vish̤ṇu cut off the head of the Asur Rāhu, when, after churning the ocean, the Surs or gods, and Asurs fought for the amr̤ita or nectar). چکردهاري चक्रधारी chakr-dhārī, A peacock, Vish̤ṇu. 3. A circular course, the lounge (in manage). چکرکهانا chakkar khānā (dakh. i. q. فريب کهانا) To be deceived or tricked. چکرمارنا chakr mārnā, To wheel round, to whirl round. گهوڙيکوچکردينا ghoṛe ko chakkar denā, To lounge a horse. 4. ( met. ) A misfortune, scrape, perplexity. 5. A side or quarter; as, چـﮩون چکرمين chahūṅ chakkar meṅ, On the four sides, on all sides. چکرورتي चक्रवर्त्ती chakr-vartī, s. m. 1. A universal monarch. 2. A title of Brāhmans in Bengal.
s. m. 1. Hair. 2. Lock of hair.
subs: (ćakra+āyudha), adj. & s.m. Fighting with the discus; — an epithet of Vishnu
s.m. 'The line which bisects a circle,' a diameter
(-kā), To whirl (a thing) rapidly round (so as to make it present the appearance of (a disc)
s.f. Circular motion, rotatory motion, rotation
adj. (f. -ī), Touching a circle, tangential; — ćakr-chūtī lakīr, s.f. A tangent
s.m. An epithet of Vishnu (lit. 'bearing a discus'); — a peacock; — a circular course; the lounge (in a manege)
v.n. To be put into rotatory motion, to whirl, turn round, revolve; to move or fly in a circle; to go a long round, take a circuitous road
v.n. To wheel round, to whirl round, to revolve, to move or fly in a circle; to take a roundabout road
s.m. Trick, fraud, deception, evasion, subterfuge, shuffle
(-ke), To be involved in difficulties, be entangled in the meshes (of)
(-ko), To involve one in difficulty or mess; to perplex, bewilder, mislead, lead astray
adv. Revolving like a wheel, in rotation

wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit चक्र (cakra). Doublet of چرخہ (carxa), چاک (cāk), چکر (cakr), and چَکَّہ (cakka).

def: s.f. A small round piece or patch (of leather, &c.); a slice; a round flat lump (as of cheese, soap, tobacco, &c.); a round plate (of metal); a shield; the hide of a rhinoceros (of which shields are made); the tail of the sheep called dumba; the crown of the head; a letter.
more: n. f. 1. Round flat lump, as a lump of tobacco or a cake of soap. 2. A round patch of leather; a washer. 3. A round plate of metal. 4. The crown of the head. Hāth kaṭāë, pāoṅ kaṭāë, kaṭāī sir kī chaktī, Zinde par murdā chaṛhe, dekh muë kī mastī! Riddle. Ans. Mashkīza , a water bag. Both hands cut off, and feet cut off, and crownless too her head, The dead upon the living ride! the bitch to lewdness wed!
s. f. 1. The hide of a rhinoceros (of which shields are made). 2. A patch of leather, a slice, compressed, flattened, a patch, the round of a cheese. 3. A round plate of metal, &c.

def: s.m. The ruddy goose, commonly called the Brāhmanī duck, Anas casarca
more: n. m. chakvī , n. f. surkhāb , S. चक्रवाक The ruddy shel-drake or Brahmini duck; casarca rutila . ☞ It is found all over India in the winter. It breeds on rocks on the borders of the great Himalayan lakes. The bird extends all over Central Europe and the greater part of Asia and Northern Africa. The Indians have a legend that two lovers for some indiscretion were turned into brahmini ducks and condemned to pass the night apart from each other on the opposite banks of a river. All night long each asks in its turn if it shall join its mate, and the answer is always in the negative. ' Chakvā , shall I come?' 'No, chakvī.' 'Chakvī , shall I come?' 'No, chakvā .'
s. m. 1. ( चक्रवाक ) Ruddy goose. (Anas casarca). 2. ( चक्र ) Whirlpool.
subs: (ćakwā+anḍa), s.m. The egg of the ćakwī

def: s.f. Motion, movement, gait, walk, carriage; pace (of a horse); a move (in chess, &c.); course, procedure, process, mode, method, manner, fashion; rule, practice, custom, usage; conduct, behaviour, habit; breeding, politeness; means, expedient, plan, scheme, device, artifice, manœuvre, trick, stratagem; a metre; a tune, an air; — a kind of fish
more: n. m. S. चल to move. 1. Motion; movement; walk; gait; carriage. 1. Pūt faqīrnī kā, chāl ahdyoṅ kī sī . Prov. A beggar woman's son, and he struts like an Ahdī! 2. Chandr badan, mirgā lochnī, hastī chāl chalaṅt, Ham tohe pūchheṅ, 'he sakhī, chhatyāṅ kāhe ḍharaṅt?' 'Maiṅ jānā mūrakh mue, mūrakh abhīṅ jiant, 'Kar lāge girvar gire, chhatyāṅ kyoṅ na ḍharant .' 'Bright as the moon, fawn eyed, with th' elephant's majesty, I ask you, sister, why your breasts now hanging lie?' 'I thought the fools were dead, one's living yet I see, Hills yield to hands, much more the breast's solidity.' 2. The paces of a horse. 3. A move (in chess, etc). 4. Practice; established practice; custom; usage; rule. 5. Process; course; procedure; method; manner. Tohre des, gorī, tosak takye, Hamre des meṅ bhuiyāṅ kī chāl . Soft beds and bolsters, lady fair, you in your country keep, Our native country's custom 'tis upon …

s. f. Gait, motion, movement, pace, procedure, habit, custom, practice, method, way. چال پکڙنا chāl pakaṛnā, To prevail. چال چلنا chāl chalnā, To behave. چال ڐهال chāl-ḍhāl, s. f. Gait, motion, procedure, manners, behaviour, breeding, politeness. چال ملنا chāl milnā, To smell a rat.
s. f. A colour in horses, roan.
subs: (-kī), To follow the course, or practice, or fashion, &c. (of); to adopt the custom, &c. (of); to become current, to prevail
vn. To behave, act, conduct oneself; to practise tricks or deception (on, -se), to behave deceitfully (towards)
(-kī), To show the paces (of); to go away, be off
s.f. Gait, motion, procedure; manners, behaviour, deportment; breeding, politeness; fashion, make, frame
(-kī), To get (at); to smell a rat
(-kī), To fall into the scheming (of), to be tricked or deceived (by).

def: s.m. Motion; departure; an auspicious moment or day for setting out on a journey (there are different days for different quarters of the compass); the departure of a bride or wife for the home of her husband; the ceremony of the wife's going to and returning from her father's home shortly alter marriage (four such visits are made; see ćār-ćāle); — stratagem, trick; misfortune, calamity.
more: n. m. 1. Departure; departure of the wife from her parent's for her husband's home. 2. The auspicious moment for setting out on a journey. 1. Āj Gaṅgā jī kā chālā hai . 2. Maṅgal, Budh uttar dis kālā, Som, Sanīchar pūrab na chālā . Tuesday and Wednesday towards the North is ruin black, Monday and Saturday Eastward is woe; alack! 3. Consummation of the marriage. 4. Trick ( buttā ). 5. Calamity; misfortune. chāle pāṛnā , v. a. To afflict; wound; oppress. Ohe re! ohe re! mhāro man hare līye jāë hai, Yeh jo jāë hai ghūṅghaṭ meṅ chāle pāṛ-ke . Rus. Dalmīr. Oh! oh! my heart is filched from me, alas! The veiled one pierced me as she me did pass.
s. m. Motion, departure. ( met. ) A lucky moment.

more: n. f. 1. Itchiness ( jhonj ). 2. Lust; heat; sexual passion. 3. Restlessness; fidget; disquietude; uneasiness. Kyā hātoṅ meṅ chul hai? chul uṭhnā , v. n. To feel an itchiness, or the sexual passion. chul miṭānā , v. a. To satisfy the sexual passion.

s. f. 1. Going, dispersion. 2. Variation, departure from truth, failure in the performance of a promise or accomplishment of a prediction. چل دينا chal denā, v. a. 1. To march (as, an army). 2. To beguile.

s.m. Motion to and fro, motion
adj. & s.m. Fickle-minded, inconstant, mutable; — a fickle person; — fickleness, mutability, &c. (see next)
s.f. = ćal-ćittatva, s.m. Fickleness of mind, unsteadiness, inconstancy, mutability, frivolity
s.m. The holy fig-tree, Ficus religiosa
(ćala+indriya), adj. & s.m. Having unsteady organs, sensitive; sensual, not having subdued senses or passions; — a sensual person, &c.
adj. & s.m. Not true to (one's) word; taunting, sneering; — a liar, a shuffler.

n. m. P. chehl ; H. chālīs forty. 1. A period of forty days; a forty days' fast; a similar period of religious seclusion, or the place where it is observed. 2. The period of forty days after child-birth during which a woman is unclean; the ceremony of purification. 3. The period of forty days in winter when the cold is severe. Dhan ke pandhrā, Makar pachīs, Chille ke din haiṅ chālīs . Prov. Fifteen days in December, and in January twenty-five. See shivering winter's reign for forty days survive. chillā bāṅdhnā , v. a. To make a vow. chillā kheṅchnā, chille baiṭhnā , v. n. To pass in a mosque, or a cell, or privacy forty days successively in fasting and divine worship. Kamāṅ-abrū, mere ghar kyoṅ na āve Ki jiske vāste kheṅche haiṅ chille?

s. m. 1. The forty days which the religious fraternities of the East sometimes pass, retired to their cells or remaining at home, in fasting and divine worship; the fast of Lent. 2. Quarantine. 3. The period of forty days after childbed, during which a woman remains unclean. چلاکهينچنا chillā khainchnā, To keep quarantine.
s. m. 1. The string of a bow. 2. Gold threads put in the border of a turband. چلاکهينچنا chillā khainchnā, 1. To bend a bow. 2. To prepare for battle. چلاچڙهانا chillā chaṛhānā, To bend a bow.
s. m. A period of forty days: (dakh.) a shrine of a saint or a faḳīrʼs residence ( Qanoon-e-islam ).
s. (dakh.) Hearth ( Harris ): see چولها chūlhā.

v.n. To go away, depart, be off; to go on or along, to proceed, to continue; to last
ćale ćalnā, v.n. To go on, be going on, to keep moving or going, to keep on (a road, walk, &c.), to continue to be or to remain (on a course, or according to a rule).

ety: S. चालनं rt. चल्; or i.q. ćālānā = ćalānā, q.v.
def: s.m. An invoice or way-bill, bill of lading; a list (of letters, &c. sent); a certificate of despatch; a remittance; a memorandum of money received and invested; a pass, a passport; clearance (of a ship); sending up or forwarding (a case, or a prisoner, &c. to a magistrate, &c.); transportation
more: n. m. S. चल् to more. 1. A list of letters sent; a certificate of despatch; invoice; way-bill; bill of lading. 2. A remittance. 3. A memorandum of money received and invested. 4. A pass; passport; clearance (of a ship). 5. A criminal case sent up to the Magistrate by the police. chālān-dār , n. The person who accompanies a despatch or remittance and has charge of the invoice; an escort. chālān kīyā jānā , v. n. 1. To be forwarded or despatched. 2. To be committed for trial or prison.
subs: s.m. The person who has charge of an invoice, the bearer of a despatch or remittance; an escort
v.t. To send up (a case, or a prisoner, &c. to a magistrate), to commit (a prisoner) for trial; to forward an invoice (of).
wety: From چَلْنا (calnā, “to walk; go”) + ـَان (-ān).

def: s.m. Motion; shaking; sifting; — a sieve; a strainer ( = ćālanī)
subs: s.m. A sifter, one who sifts.

def: s.m. Going, proceeding, departing; course, procedure, process; gait, carriage; manner of life, conduct, behaviour, mien, deportment; habit, custom; ceremony; way, fashion, manner, mode; currency (of money); method, management (of expenditure, &c.), economy; — adj. Tremulous, shaking; unsteady; customary, fashionable, current
more: n. m. S. चल् to move. 1. Way of life; conduct; habits; character. 2. Course; process; procedure. 3. Fashion; cut; mode; manner. 4. Currency; circulation; legal tender. Angrezī rupaë kā chalan hai . 5. Moderation in expenditure; frugality; economy. Woh baṛe chalan kā ādmī hai. chalan se chalnā , v. n. To be frugal, economical. bhalā chalan , n. m. Good behaviour, conduct, or character. khoṭā yā burā chalan , n. m. Bad conduct; misdemeanor.
1. s. m. Habit, custom, process, course, fashion, conduct, behaviour. 2. Ceremony. 3. Currency. 4. adj. Current. چلن چلنا chalan chalnā, To behave.
subs: v.n. To proceed in a course, &c.; to behave
adv. After a good fashion or manner, with propriety, carefully; methodically, with good management, economically
adj. & s.m. About to go or depart; about to decamp or abscond; about to die, moribund; — one who is about to depart, &c.
wety: From Sanskrit चलन (calana).

ety: ćalā˚ = Prk. चलाव(इ), or चलावे(इ) = S. चल+आपय(ति)
def: v.t. To cause to move or go or pass, to move, put in motion, set agoing; to stir, agitate (with a spoon, &c.); to hasten, urge, impel, propel, drive; to discharge (an arrow or gun), to fire (a gun or cannon), to shoot (at, -par), to throw (a dart, stone, &c.); — to originate, introduce, bring into use; to promulgate; to accustom; to make current, give currency to, put in circulation, to issue, utter (as coin, &c.), pass; — to transmit, send, forward; to advance or lend (money, — e.g. sūd-par rupayā ćalānā); to do, act, transact, carry on, conduct, manage, direct (a work, business, &c.); to prosecute, pursue, carry out; to put in execution, to bring into effect, to enforce; to use, employ, apply; — to thrust (into), drive (in)
more: v. n. S. चित्कार a scream. 1. To scream; screech; squeak; squeal; cry out; exclaim; make a noise; bawl; bellow; roar; howl; yell. 1. Chillāī ki 'haëṅ! kidhar gayā gul,' Jhunjlāī ki 'kaun de gayā jul?' Nasīm. 2. Chillāte chillāte galā paṛ gayā . 2. To cry for help; wail; complain. 3. To rage; to flare or howl at. Ham par nāhaq chillāte haiṅ .

v. a. S. चल to move. See chalnā . 1. To set in motion; set agoing; grind. 2. To stir; agitate (with a spoon); churn. 3. To impel; drive; direct; urge. 4. To conduct; manage; administer; guide; direct; lead. 5. To forward; advance; hasten; expedite. 6. To issue; pass; utter; circulate; make current. 7. To put in execution; bring into effect; enforce. 8. To prosecute; pursue; carry out. 9. To spread; disseminate; propagate; publish. 10. To transmit; send down (one's name). 11. To speak of. 1. Bhāī-band, kuṭam, kabīlā, mātā, pitā, sut, dhīr, Mulak kī kaun chalāve, saṅg na chalego sarīr . Brethren or kindred, wife, sire, mother, son, What need to speak of them, your own flesh will you shun. 2. Ajī! inkī bhalī chalāī! My dear Sir! nothing is too strange for him . 12. To let; lend at interest; invest money. Sūd par rupayā chalānā . 13. To bring into use; to use; employ; apply. 14. To penetrate…
v. a. To distil, cause to drip, &c. See چوانا choʼānā.
v. n. To scream, shriek, to roar, bawl, bellow, cry out, exclaim.

( चालन or caus. of چلنا q. v.) v. a. 1. To impel, set a going, make to go or pass, to drive, actuate, direct. 2. To fire a musket or cannon, to shoot at, to do, hasten, forward, send, accustom, act, advance, utter, throw, thrust, use, walk.
v. a. To discharge (an arrow), to shoot.

Walk not this earth, O man, with such a lofty swell and air, Men walk this earth but to their graves, beware and walk with care.

def: adj. Forty
more: adj. S. चत्वारिंशत् Pr. chatālīsā . 1. Forty. Wohī man, wohī chālīs ser . Prov. Call it a maund, call it forty seers. (Six of one and half a dozen of the other). 2. Weighty; important.
subs: adj. (f. -ī), Of forty sers; weighty; pure, unadulterated; heavy, stupid
adj. & s.m. Having many (lit. forty) pillars; — a pavilion; a palace, &c.

def: s.m. The fortieth year of any era, or of the century; an aggregate of forty; a period of forty days; a quarantine; — a hero or winner of forty fights; — the great famine (in India) of 1783 (Sambat 1840); — a dimness of sight which is supposed to come on at forty years of age (and often to be removed on approaching the forty-eighth or fiftieth year); — adj. Numbering forty years.
more: n. m. 1. A period of forty days; quarantine (Mah. qalantīnyā ); the great famine in Hindustan of 1783 ( Sambat 1840). 2. A champion who has been successful in forty fights. chālīs'vāṅ , adj. Fortieth. chālīsvāṅ , n. m. The 40th day (after the death of a relation when food is distributed.) Mah. chālīs-serā , adj. 1. Full (forty seers). Chālīs-serī bāt kahte hain . Prov. His words are weighty (full forty seers). 3. Pure; unadulterated ( ghī ). 4. Foolish; stupid. chālīs sitūn , n. A pavilion; a palace, etc. having forty pillars.

s. m. 1. A dimness of sight which is supposed to come on at forty years of age, and often to be removed on approaching the forty-eighth or fiftieth year. 2. The fortieth year of any era, or of the century.

def: s.f. Elegant gait, graceful motion; stateliness; manner, conduct; — exertion in battle; battle.
more: s. f. 1. Exertion in battle. 2. A pompous or graceful gait.

def: s.f. Quarantine; a period of forty days abstinence (kept after the death of a kinsman, or after a childbirth, &c.).
more: s. f. Quarantine kept after the death of a relation, childbirth, &c.

more: adj. 1. Fortieth; (hence) 2. The fortieth day after childbirth, death of a relation, &c.

def: adj. & s.m. Clever, artful, astute; roguish, wicked, mischievous; deceitful, treacherous; — an artful person, &c.
more: adj. 1. Clever ( chātur ). 2. Deceitful; fraudulent; treacherous. Nāz o adā se saiṅkṛoṅ kī dil lubhā līye, Chhoṭe se sin meṅ yār baṛe tum ho chālye . Amānat. With glance and grace some thousand hearts your wiles have drawn, So young in years and so accomplished in your dawn!

ety: ćāl˚ = Prk. चाल(इ), or चाले(इ) = S. चालय(ति), caus. of rt. चल्

more: 1. v. a. To sift. 2. v. n. To be mischievous. چالن هار chālan-hār, A sifter.

ety: Prk. चलणअं; S. चलनीयं, rt. चल्
def: v.n. To move, stir; to go, proceed; to depart; to walk, travel; to flow, run (as a stream, a pen, &c.); to be loose (as the bowels); to blow (as wind); to sail (as a ship); to be discharged, be fired, to go off (as a gun); to be used, be brandished (as a sword); to set out, to start; to begin, be begun, be introduced; to pass (as coin), to be current, be in vogue, be in operation, be in force; to walk (in, -par, a path or course), to conduct or comport oneself, to behave; to go on well, to flourish, thrive (as a business, &c.); to progress, advance; to go beyond, to exceed (due bounds); to go on, continue (from some past time), be transmitted or handed down; to last, endure; to go down, be taken (as medicine); to pass, work, answer, serve, be of avail, to avail (against); to try tricks or pranks (with or on, -se), to practise trickery or deceit (on); — to move (mentally towards, -par, with the desire of possessing, &c.); — to go, give way, slip, begin to fall; to begin to wear out; — to be made out, be deciphered (as the letters, writing, &c.); — to be thrust (in), be driven (into)
more: v. n. S. चलनं going. Pr. chālai he trembles. 1. To move; go; go on; pass; stir. 1. Gāṛī * ko ukhṛi † kaheṅ, aur naqad māl ‡ ko khoyā, ¶ Chaltī ko gāṛī kaheṅ, ye dekh Kabīrā royā . Kabīr. 2. Chale rāṅḍ kā char/?/khā, aur bure kā pet . Prov. Loose bowels and the widow's spinning wheel go night and day. 3. Chaltī chākī dekh-kar dīyā Kabīrā roë, Do pāṭan bich āë-ke sābat bachā na koë . Kabīr. Kabīr has oft times wept to see the millstone going round, Whate'er between these stones hath come ne'er came out whole and sound. 2. To go or take one's way; march; move on. Ṭuk dekh līyā, dil shād kīyā, khush-vaqt huë, aur chal nikle . Pure love. Nazīr. A moment seen, my heart made glad, the bliss enjoyed, I took my way. 3. To travel; journey; start; set sail. 1. Mujhe ussho/?/kh chanchal ne jab apnā husn dikhlāyā, Dikhā-kar ek nazar chaltā huā aur mujh ko tarsā- yā . Nazīr. A moment but the skittish …

v. n. To move, go, proceed, walk, go off, pass (as, coin), be discharged (a gun, &c.), to blow, flow, drive, behave, sail, work, answer, succeed, avail, last, serve, stand. چل نکلنا chal nikalnā, To turn out vitious, to exceed bounds. چلا جانا chalā jānā, To go along, be off, depart. چلي چلنا chale chalnā, To go along.
subs: v.n. To leave and abide elsewhere; to depart this life, to die; to come to an end, to be over

v.n. To set out, to start, &c.; — to set in; to come into season, be in season or in the market (as fruit, &c.): ćal-jānā, v.n. (intens. of ćalnā), To pass, work, answer, succeed, avail; to be loose (as the bowels); to go, give way, to fall; to be worn out; to go bad, be spoiled, to turn (as food, milk, &c.); to go mad, lose (one's mind; e.g. dil ćal-gayā)
v.n. To go away, to be off, to decamp
v.n. To move on or away, to march off, to go or take one's way; — to exceed bounds, to turn out vicious.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀘𑀮𑀤𑀺 (caladi), from Sanskrit चलति (calati), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *čálHati, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *čálHati, from Proto-Indo-European *kʷél-e-ti, from *kʷel- (“to move; to turn (around)”). Compare Persian چلیدن (čalīdan, “to go; to walk”), Persian چالاک (čâlâk, “fast, agile, clever”).

more: adj. 1. Flowing; running Chaltā pānī . 2. Used by travellers. Rasta nahīṅ chaltā . 3. Now passing; current. 4. Current; in vogue. 5. Customary; ordinary; usual; every-day. 6. Dextrous; clever; active. Chaltā ādmī . 7. Efficient; potent (charm). Kyā pūchhtā hai tū amal-i-bugz o muhabbat, Chaltā huā tāvīz samajh naqsh-i-diram ko . Zauq. D' you ask for amulet for love and hate, Know there's no spell like wealth to captivate. 8. Quick; lively (music). chaltā chhappar , n. m. lit . a moving roof. An umbrella. Kyoṅ bābā! koī chaltā chhappar mile to faqīr dhūp se bach jāë! Āzād. chaltā kharch , n. m. Current expenses. chaltā rakhnā , v. n. 1. To protract; extend. 2. To maintain; keep up; continue; bear on. chaltā kām , n. m. 1. Any work performed hurriedly and carelessly; scamped work; bad work. 2. A temporary work. chaltā k . v. a. 1. adv. With the arms and legs in working condition, while the limbs are serviceable

def: s.f. Activity, briskness (of business, trade, &c.); access, reach; sphere of action, power, authority, influence, jurisdiction; competency; — land under cultivation ( = ćaltī zamīn).
more: adj. 1. Moving; travelling; in motion. 2. Fast; quick; rapid; lively (song). 3. (Opp. of paṛtī .) Lands under cultivation. 4. Vague; loose; inexact; uncertain. Chaltī bāt . 5. Potent; powerful; effective.

n. f. 1. Access; reach; hold; grasp; grip. 2. Competency; jurisdiction; authority; power; influence; ascendancy. Apnī chaltī meṅ kaun kasar kartā hai? 3. Active trade. Āj kal kām kī chaltī hai. chalat phirat , n. f. Movement; motion; manœuvre. chalte phirte nazar āo , intj. Be off; go away. chalte chalte , adj. 1. While going; on the way. 2. Gradually; by degrees. chaltī dūkān , n. f. A thriving business. chaltī zamīn . Land under cultivation. chaltī kasar ; A. kasar-i-mutwālī . ( Math .) Recurring decimals. chaltī ghirnī . ( Mech .) A moveable pulley.

def: s.m. A handful or mouthful of water ( = ćullū, q.v.); rinsing the mouth by sipping water from the hand and spitting it out again (a ceremony performed before religious ceremonies, meals, &c.; syn. āćaman).
more: Gaṛh. n. m. S. चुलुक. The palm of the hand hollowed for holding liquids. 1. Zāhid! sharāb pīne se kāfir huā maiṅ kyoṅ? Kyā ḍeṛh chullū pānī meṅ īmān bah gayā? Gālib. O man devout, wherefore an infidel for drinking wine? Can faith be drowned in inches two of grape juice of the vine? 2. Chullū pānī taṅg zindgānī . Prov. A drop of water just to get, to live hard-set. chullū bhar . A handful of water. chullū chullū sādhnā , v. a. 1. To sip (Sant. sirup ). Chullū chullū sādhegā to loṭe bhar par āvegā . Prov. A wee drop if you take you 'll manage soon a pot of sack. 2. To become rich by adding on a little and a little more. Chullū chullū sādhegā to duāre hāthī bāṅdhegā . Prov. A little join to a little more, an elephant waits at your door. chullū se pīnā , v. n. To drink water out of the palm of the hand. chullū lenā , Hin.; kullī k. , Mah. v. a. To rinse one's mouth after eating. chullū meṅ u…

s. m. A handful, the palm of the hand contracted so as to hold water. چلوبهرپاني مين ڐوب مرنا chullū bhar pānī meṅ ḍūb marnā (lit. To drown oneʼs self in a handful of water) To be greatly abashed: see چپني بهرپاني مين ڐوب مرنا chapnī bhar pānī meṅ ḍūb marnā. چلوچلوسادهنا chullū chullū sādhnā, To get a habit of drinking by gradual increase. چلومين الوهونا chullū meṅ ullū honā, To be intoxicated with a mouthful.

def: s.m. The hand hollowed to hold a little water, &c.; a handful of water ( = ćullū); a small pot, a gallipot.

more: s. f. A kind of fish. (Clupea cultrata: Buch. mss. ).

def: adj. & s.m. Moving about much, wandering about, roving; — one who moves about a great deal, a great walker, &c.

v.t. = ćalānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to move or go or pass, to move, put in motion, set agoing; to stir, agitate (with a spoon, &c.); to hasten, urge, impel, propel, drive; to discharge (an arrow or gun), to fire (a gun or cannon), to shoot (at, -par), to throw (a dart, stone, &c.); — to originate, introduce, bring into use; to promulgate; to accustom; to make current, give currency to, put in circulation, to issue, utter (as coin, &c.), pass; — to transmit, send, forward; to advance or lend (money, — e.g. sūd-par rupayā ćalānā); to do, act, transact, carry on, conduct, manage, direct (a work, business, &c.); to prosecute, pursue, carry out; to put in execution, to bring into effect, to enforce; to use, employ, apply; — to thrust (into), drive (in)
more: v. a. Caus. of chalānā , q. v.

def: s.f.m.(?) = ćāl-ćalan, q.v.s.v. ćāl.
s.m. Skin, hide; leather
more: n. m. S. चर्म्म, P. chirm . Hide; skin; leather ( chamṛā ). 1. Kām piyārā hai, chām piyārā nahīṅ . Prov. The work you give is sweet, not your good looks. (Handsome is that handsome does). 2. Nain jharoke mār le, darshan hī se kām, Tulsī, woh nar kūṛ haiṅ jo ghiseṅ chām se chām . Tulsī Dās. Love glances from the windows cast for the pleasure of the eye, The man's a fool, O Tulsī, who mere skin on skin will ply. chām-chiṛakh , n. f. lit . leather-winged. The bat. chām-chor , n. m. An adulterer. chām-chorī , n. f. Adultery. chām ke dām chalānā , v. a. 1. To introduce a leather currency. 2. To stretch to the utmost one's brief authority. ☞ The story is that a Bhishtī , called Nizām, having saved the emperor Humāyūṅ from drowning. with the aid of a masak , he was desired to name his reward. His saviour would be content with four hours' absolute authority as Emperor. In this short period he is…
s. m. Hide, skin, leather. چام کي دام چلاني chām ke dām chalāne, v. a. To stretch to the utmost a temporary authority. ( lit. To give currency to a coin of leather).
subs: s.f. Adulterous connexion with another man's wife
s.m. The leather money made current by the bhishtī, Nizām, during his three days rule in the time of the Emperor Humāyūn
To introduce a leather currency; to stretch to the utmost a temporary authority.

ety: ćamak˚ = Prk. चमक्क(इ) or चमक्के(इ), S. चमत्+क्रियते, pass. fr. कृ
def: v.n. To shine, beam, glow, glimmer, sparkle, glisten, glitter, glare; to be bright, be polished; — to do well, prosper, flourish, thrive; to be lively or brisk (as a market, &c.); to prevail, rage (as an epidemic, &c.); to fly into a passion, become angry; — to be startled, to start, shy; — to get to the head (liquor)
wety: Inherited from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀘𑀫𑁆𑀫𑀓𑁆𑀓 (*cammakka), from an onomatopoeic 𑀘𑀫𑁆𑀫 (camma) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓𑁆𑀓- (-kka-). Related to چونکنا (cauṅknā). Cognate with Punjabi ਚਮਕਣਾ (camkaṇā) / چَمَکنا (camaknā), Gujarati ચમકવું (camakvũ), Marathi चमकणे (ċamakṇe).

To cause to glitter, cause to beam or glow; to flash; to burnish, brighten, polish; to display, to make a show; to make famous; to cause to flourish or prosper, to extend, to carry to a great height of prosperity; to wave, brandish, flourish; to rouse, stir up, make lively or active; to provoke, to cause to start or shy, to startle
s.f. = ćampak-baṛnī, q.v.

ety: ćamkā(nā)+āʼo = Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं; and -āhaṭ = āwaṭ = aʼo or āw+Prk. अंट्टं = S. क+त्वं; and -āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका
def: Brightness, glittering, glitter, much light, gleam, flash, brilliance, lustre, splendour; — flourish; — starting, start.

v.t. To cause to glitter, cause to beam or glow; to flash; to burnish, brighten, polish; to display, to make a show; to make famous; to cause to flourish or prosper, to extend, to carry to a great height of prosperity; to wave, brandish, flourish; to rouse, stir up, make lively or active; to provoke, to cause to start or shy, to startle
more: v. n. 1. To beam; glow; flash. 2. To be polished; to glitter. 1. Tāl meṅ chamke tāl-machharyā, ran chamke taruār, Taṅbuā chamke saiyāṅ pagaṛyā, sej pe biṅdyā hamār . The fishes glisten in the pond, and swords upon the battle field, My husband's turban in the tent, my spangles' sheen in bed revealed. 2. Na gauhar meṅ hai woh, na hai saṅg meṅ, Valekin chamaktā hai har raṅg meṅ . Mīr Hasan. Not in rich jewels bright, nor in stone e'en, In every hue and form God's image's seen. 2. To be startled; to shy ( bidaknā ). Ghoṛā hāthī se chamaktā hai . 3. To do well; thrive; prosper; flourish. 4. To be lively (the market), in good demand. Bāzār chamak rahā hai . 5. To break out; to be prevalent; to rage. Haizā chamak rahā hai . 6. To flare or fire up. Chuṭkiyāṅ leṅ aur, chamkeṅ ham par! 7. To get to the head. Ab nashā chamaktā ātā hai .
v. n. 1. To glitter, shine, flash, glare, startle. 2. To prosper. 3. To be angry.
wety: Inherited from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀘𑀫𑁆𑀫𑀓𑁆𑀓 (*cammakka), from an onomatopoeic 𑀘𑀫𑁆𑀫 (camma) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓𑁆𑀓- (-kka-). Related to چونکنا (cauṅknā). Cognate with Punjabi ਚਮਕਣਾ (camkaṇā) / چَمَکنا (camaknā), Gujarati ચમકવું (camakvũ), Marathi चमकणे (ċamakṇe).

more: n. m. S. चणक A grain, commonly known as gram; Cicer arietinum . 1. Jo koī khāe chane kā ṭūk, pānī pīve sau sau ghūṅṭ . Prov. Who fried peas eat at all will gulp down water draught on draught. 2. Are chaṛāṅ to mhārā jītab saë! Rus. Why, gram is the food on which I live! 3. Chanā mard nāj hai . Gram is strengthening. Prov. 4. Kabhī ghī ghanā, kabhī muṭṭhī bhar chanā, kabhī voh bhī manā . Prov. Now of butter plenty, now of gram a fistful, now even it without. 5. Chaṛāṅ kahe merī ūṅchī nāk, Ek ghar daliye do ghar rāṛ, Jo khāve merā ek ṭūk, Pārīṅ pīve sau sau ghūnṭ . Rus. Prov. Gram says my dignity bespeaks my nose so high, The mill in this house goes, to that the sound doth fly, Though little ate great gulps of water in you 'll lie. chane yā chane se bhūnnā , v. a. To fire a volley. Ek bāṛ meṅ chane se bhūn dīye. chane se bhunnā, chane bhunnā , v. n. lit . to be roasted as gram. To be in a h…

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Moving to and fro, movable, trembling, shaking, tremulous; unsteady, restless, unstable, inconstant, inconsiderate, changeful, volatile, fickle, uncertain; heedless, lawless; wanton, sportive, playful; transient, perishable; — an unsteady character, an inconstant or fickle person, &c.; a libertine
more: adj. S. चञ्चल See achpal 1, 2, 3. 1. Chanchal nār chhail se laṛī, Ghaṛī āṅgan, ghaṛī bāhar khaṛī . The skittish sprite with her gallant doth fight, Now in the yard, now in the open quite. 2. Chanchal nār kī chāl chhipe nahīṅ, nīch chhipe na baṛappan pāë, Jogī ko bhek anek dharo koī, karam chhipe na bhabūt ramāë . The wanton's ways hid ne'er can be, no height can baseness hide, Nor evil deeds in saintly garb, nor ashes free applied.
adj. 1. Trembling, tremulous. 2. Restless, wanton, playful, fickle. 3. Perishable, transitory.
subs: adj. Of uncertain disposition, capricious, fickle, false-hearted.

ety: Prk. चन्दो or चंदो; S. चन्द्रः
def: s.m. The moon; the crown of the head; a white spot in the forehead of cattle, &c. (and many other things which are moon-like); a semi-lunar gold ornament (worn under two others on the head); a target or mark (to shoot at); a lunar month
more: n. m. S. चन्द्र, Pr. chando . 1. The moon. 1. Chāṅd ko gahan lag gayā . Prov. The moon is eclipsed. 2. Khāk ḍāle chāṅd nahīṅ chhuptā . Prov. The moon is not hid by throwing dust on her. (Solid worth is not sullied by slander). 3. Chāṅd chaṛhe, kul ālam dekhe . Prov. All the world can see the moon in the sky. 4. Chor kā muṅh chānd sā . Prov. The thief's countenance is as smooth as the moon. (His designs are veiled). 5. Dīyā to chāṅdithā, na dīyā to mūṅh māṅd thā . Prov. Give, and you behold the brightness of the moon; withhold, and the face before you is dull. 6. Baṛe shahar kā baṛā chāṅd . Prov. Great cities, big moon (dignitaries. Ironic. Swindlers). 7. Chandā māmūṅ dūr ke, baṛe pakvāveṅ būr ke, Ap khāeṅ thālī meṅ, ham ko deṅ pālī meṅ . Boys' rhyme. Our uncle moon shines from afar, husk balls he fries for peas, For him a golden plate, for us an earthen pot apiece. 8. Ek āṅkh, us par bhī…

s. m. 1. The moon. 2. The crown of the head. 3. A white spot in the forehead of cattle, and many other things which are moon-like. 4. Name of an ornament. 5. A month. 6. A target (to shoot at). چاندچهپانا chānd chhipānā, To change as the moon. چاندرات chānd-rāt, End of the month. چاند تارا chānd-tārā, A robe made of flowering muslin, &c. چاندمارنا chānd mārnā, To fire at a mark. چاندني کهيت کيا chānd ne khet kiyā, The moon has risen.
s. A semilunar golden ornament worn under two others on the head ( Qanoon-e-islam ).
subs: s.m. Cloth figured with moons and stars; a robe made of flowered muslin, &c.

To fire at a target or mark

be raised to a post of honour; — to make a display, to act or walk ostentatiously

def: adj. How much? how many? how often? how long? — so many; several, some, a few, sundry; (in comp.) quantity, as much or many, times, fold (e.g. du-ćand, 'two-fold')

adj. How many? how much? how often? some, a few: (in compos. quantity, as much, many). چنددرچند chand dar chand, adj. Several, various. چند روزه chand-roza, adj. Of a few days, ephemeral. هرچند har-chand or هرچندکه har-chand-ki, adv. Although, nevertheless, notwithstanding, however, with all that, so much the more.

more: n. m. P. chand few. 1. Quota; subscription; contribution. 2. Assessment; in police accounts, the fund for remounts. chandā k. , v. a. To raise a subscription.

ety: for ćanda, rel. n. fr. ćand, q.v.
def: s.m. Contribution, subscription, donation; assessment; — the fund for remounts (in police accounts)
subs: To pay a contribution, give a subscription or donation

def: s.m. = ćanḍāl, q.v.
s.m. contrac. of ćanḍāl, q.v.
s.m. The most degraded of the mixed tribes; a man of this tribe (born from a Śūdra father and a Brāhman mother); an outcast, a miscreant, a vile wretch, a malignant or diabolical creature; a miser
more: s. m. A man of the lowest Hindū cast; ( met. ) an outcast, wretch, miser.
subs: s.m. A hair on the head (considered unlucky)
s.m. A company of four or more miscreants.

def: s.f. A woman of the ćanḍāl caste; the wife of a ćanḍāl; a degraded vile woman, &c.
s.f. A woman of the ćanḍāl caste; the wife of a ćanḍāl; a degraded vile woman, &c.
